rationale: All five image_load events (A0, EV-001 through EV-004) show DismHost.exe loading a consistent set of Microsoft-signed DISM provider DLLs (DismCorePS.dll, DismProv.dll, OSProvider.dll, LogProvider.dll, DmiProvider.dll) within a 77ms window. Every loaded image carries a valid Microsoft Windows signature, correct file versions (10.0.17134.1), and matching original file names, consistent with a legitimate Windows servicing/update operation. DismHost.exe running from a GUID-named subdirectory under Windows\Temp is standard Windows Update Agent behavior when staging DISM operations. No unsigned, renamed, or anomalous DLLs are present, and no process creation, network, or registry events suggesting abuse of this pattern (e.g., DLL hijacking or sideloading) are included in the package.
